Details:
A powdered activated carbon isotherm study showed a relatively low adsorption capacity for methomyl by PAC. The study examined the correlation between octanol-water partition coefficients and adsorption and found that pesticides with higher polarity (or lower Kow values) are more difficult to remove.
Studies investigating the adsorption capacity of non-ionic, hypercrosslinked polymeric resins to adsorb methomyl from aqueous solution found that the effectiveness of the polymer ranged substantially depending on the characteristics of the polymer’s functional group.
